STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q S treet• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Scott E. Porter 
Jones & Mayer 
City Attorney 
City of Fullerton 
3777 North Harbor Blvd. 
Fullerton, CA 92835 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-203 
Dear Mr. Porter: 
August 28. 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Councilmcmber Greg Sebourn 
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we arc only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section l 090. Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (111 re 
Oglesby ( 1975) l FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts arc complete and 
accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should 
contact us for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Docs Council member Sebourn have a conlfo.:t of inlc1 csl \\ ith respect lo a <lcc1siun tu 1 l!nc,,· 
or cancel membership in the Southern California Association of Governments (SCAG) based on his 
employment with Stantec Consulting Services Inc. ("'Stantec"), a company that is a contractor to 
SCAG? 
CONCLUSION 
No. The Councilmembcr has no financial interest in SCAG and the SCAG dceision will not 
have a foreseeable financial effect on Stantec. Therefore. the Councilmcmber will not have a 
conflict of interest in the decision to renew or cancel membership in SCAG. 
1 The Pohucal Reform Act is contained in Go,·emment Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are lo the Go\'emment Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulauons of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 181 IO through 18997 of Title 2 of tlte California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Di, 1s1on 6 of the California Code of Regulauons. unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-17-203 
Page No. 2 
Council member Sebourn is one of fi\'e members of the Fullerton City Council. In his private 
capacity, Councilmembcr Sebourn is employed by Stantec.2 According to Stantec's website: 
·'Our work-engineering. architecture, interior design, landscape architecture, 
surveying, environmental sciences, construction services, project management, 
and project economics, from initial project concept and planning through to 
design, construction, commissioning, maintenance, decommissioning, and 
remediation-begins at the intersection of community, creativity, and 
relationships." 
Stantcc has approximately 22,000 employees and is listed on the New York Stock exchange. 
However, Councilmember Sebourn docs not own any Stantec stock or stock options. The 
Council member docs not sit on its board of directors or hold any official titte within the 
corporation, although he oversees a small team of professionals within his particular local otlicc on 
certain surveying projects. 
One of Stantec's clients is SCAG. Approximately three Stantec employees (including the 
Councilmember) help SCAG to evaluate and manage the issuance of grants to local government 
entities. The value ofStantec's contract with SCAG is less than $100,000 per year. 
As part of the FY 2017-2018 budget adoption, Fullerton budgeted SCAG membership 
dues in the amount of approximately S 14,000. and the City paid a membership invoice the first 
week of July, 2017. At the August 15,2017 City Council meeting, the Council discussed whether 
and when the City should tenninatc its membership in the SCAG, and under what tcm1s. To avoid 
any appearance of impropriety, Councilmembcr Sebourn recused himself from the agcndi✓.ed 
discussion of the item at the August 15. 2017 Council meeting. It is expected that at the September 
19, 2017 Council meeting. the Council will again discuss whether and when to tcnnina(c the City's 
membership in SC' AG. 
You stated that you bclie\'C Councilmcmbcr Sebourn and his family have no financial 
interests in SC AG. The only connection the Councilmembcr has to SC AG is that the 
Councilmembcr is employed by Stantec, and one of Stantec's clients is SCAG. Council member 
Sebourn docs not believe the City's ongoing membership in SCAG has any impact on the 
willinuness of SC AG to continue to hire Stantec for consul ti nu work. Mr. Sebourn is not aware of 
-
-
any ''inside deals" or any other reason that Stantcc would see any additional consulting work from 
SCAG regardless of the City Council's determination. Mr. Scboum·s total compensation would not 
be affected regardless of whether Fullerton remains a member of SCAG. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making. participating in making. or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
d In the Cw 11e_1· ,\d\·il,:c kttcr :\-l 6-073. It \\·a, noted that the Councilmcmhcr 1e a licensed Profcfsionul I and 
Sur\'cyor by trade. 


I 
File No. A-1 7-203 
Page No. 3 
(Section 87103.) Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are defined in Section 87103 
and include the following: 
• 
Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on tenns available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating S 500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
Under Section 87103(c) and (d), the Councilmember has a potentially disqualifying interest 
in Stantec. Moreover, income of an official includes a pro rata share of the income of a business 
entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns. directly. indirectly, or beneficially, a I 0 
percent interest or greater. (Section 82030(a).) However. the Councilmember has no ownership 
interest in Stantcc, thus he has no interest in the sources of income to Stantec (including SCAG). 
Foresc>c>ability: The standard for foreseeability differs depending on whether an interest is 
explicitly involved in the decision. Under your facts, Stantec is not explicitly involved in the 
decision. Thus, as applied to Stantec. Regulation 1870 I (b) pro\'ides: "In general, if the financial 
effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is 
reasonably foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary 
circumstances not subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably foreseeable." 
Materiality: Regulation 18702.3(a)(4) provides for a source of income that is a business 
entity, "the financial effect is material if a prudent person with sufficient infonnation would find it 
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision· s financial effect would contribute to a change in the 
price of the business entity's publicly traded stock . . .. " 
The City Council decision will dctem1ine whether the City renews or cancels its 
membership in SCAG. While Stantcc has a husincss relationship with SCAG. there .ire no factǊ to 
indicate that the financial effect of the decisions regarding SCAG would contribute to a change in 
the price of Stante«;'s publicly traded stock. Therefore, the Councilmcmber-would not ha\'e a 
conflict of interest. 
If you have other questions on this matter. please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely. 
Jack C. Woodside 
nt General Counsel 
i\•ision
